TextENGNew: It wasn't her fault.
She had fought against fate with earnest sincerity and remarkable courage.
Because she wanted to protect what was precious to her. Because she couldn't bear to lose it.
That was her powerful, noble kindness. And her love.
...But then she realized.
In wanting so desperately to love someone,
she had forgotten how to let herself be loved -- and found herself utterly alone.
``You could have done something for him, Rika-chan, but you didn't!!``
...Rena's words from that time came flooding back. The shock was like being doused with ice water. In that instant... I truly froze, unable to muster a single word in response.
Those words were an indictment of my failure to act a year ago, when I could have. And now, all I could do was look back on those past memories as scars.
Regret, disappointment, and guilt beyond what words could express. They tormented me still, and every time I saw Satoko remembering Satoshi, I felt that heavy, piercing pain.
If only I had possessed the power to shatter fate back then... no, if only I had possessed the resolve and the will.
The tragedy Rena had tried to bring about should never have been a possibility in the first place--
``......Is that true?``
The tragedy involving Rena, in that other world.
A sorrowful world born from Rena's desperate attempt to protect her family.
She murdered Rina Mamiya, the woman who had been running a marriage scam to swindle her father out of everything, and then she even killed Rina's accomplice, Teppei Houjou, with her own hands.
And the story didn't end there... Her guilt and remorse festered into dark, obsessive paranoia directed at her friends... ultimately cascading into yet more tragedy.
``......I suppose... that isn't an impossible story.@k@vS14/01/210100764. If people like that were destroying my family, I might think the same way.``
``And... if you committed a sin and tried to hide it without ever atoning... the anxiety and paranoia would probably drive you mad.``
``Mii. ...You believe me?``

``...Well, I don't know about 'fate,' but I believe in the possibility.@k@vS14/01/210100767. The future you're predicting could very well become reality, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/01/210100768. So isn't it only natural?``
When I told him about it on the walk back from the coffee shop, Keiichi believed me yet again.
...Disarmingly simple. And hopelessly kindhearted.
But.
That gentle receptiveness, like cotton absorbing water... was what I was most grateful for, more than anything.
``Then we'll need to give Rena a heads-up about this.``
``...Oh, Rika-chan?``
``What is it?``
``Don't tell me you're thinking...@k@vS14/01/210100772. that we should just look the other way again?``
``Mii... I'm not that heartless.``
I puffed out my cheeks and glared at Keiichi. ...I knew it was a joke, but it still irked me. That Shion-like persistence of his -- I really wished he'd grow out of it.
``For starters, it's better to share the truth with her.@k@vS14/05/210500542. ...However.``
I found myself voicing the concern that had been nagging at my heart.
``If Rena truly cherishes her family...@k@vS14/05/210500544. will she really listen calmly to what we have to say?``
``Huh? ...What do you mean by that?``
``Honestly, I think Rena dislikes Rina.@k@vS14/05/210500546. ...But when I saw them together at Angel Mort, she was hiding those feelings to protect her father's happiness.``
``So if Rena were to learn that Rina was actually deceiving her father... how do you think she'd react?``
The more I talked through my own reasoning, the larger my anxiety grew.
Rena was normally bright, cheerful, and easygoing... but when provoked, her emotions would erupt like a raging inferno, burning away every last shred of self-control.
She was every bit as quick to act as Keiichi, but to put it bluntly, she had the strongest tendency to spiral out of control.
If I recalled correctly, Rena blamed herself for her parents' divorce and carried that guilt with her.
Her home had fallen apart because she hadn't tried hard enough to protect it... I seemed to remember her saying something like that in a previous world.
...If so, then the moment Rena learned a terrible woman was deceiving her father, wouldn't she try to erase that woman by any means necessary in order to protect him?
Which would mean that we ourselves could be the ones pushing Rena toward tragedy...
``There's a saying about pouring oil on a fire.@k@vS14/05/210500549. If we tell her about this, don't you think Rena's hostility could transform straight into murderous intent...?``
``That's exactly why we need to talk it through properly.@k@vS14/01/210100775. I'm sure even Rena can understand that killing her enemy won't solve anything -- that it would only bring more misery.``
``......Keiichi is far too optimistic.``
``Or maybe you're just too pessimistic, Rika-chan?``
He has no idea how worried I am... How carefree of him.
``Besides--``
Perhaps sensing my displeasure, Keiichi dropped his smile and spoke to me more seriously.
``Rena can be surprisingly stubborn once she gets something in her head, you know.@k@vS14/01/210100779. ...Or rather, it's more like she convinces herself that's how things are.``
``Convinces... herself?``
``Yeah.@k@vS14/01/210100781. You've seen how Rena gets about her collection, or the lunches she makes, or her 'kyute' mode... she really leans into projecting that persona, right?``
``But sometimes she takes it too far, and any deviation from that ideal becomes unacceptable -- like she'll think no one would accept her otherwise.``
``...When it's really just something she's convinced herself of.@k@vS14/01/210100784. She doesn't have to curate her image like some idol.``
``......Is that so?``
I hadn't noticed that at all. Keiichi had been observing that closely all this time.
Like an idol... When he put it that way, it made sense. We all have sides of ourselves we'd rather hide, but we don't feel ashamed of that. Because it's perfectly natural.

But for some reason, Rena demanded purity of herself. She strove endlessly to be the good girl. ...And that was precisely what had led to the past tragedy -- going off on her own rampage without confiding in anyone.

``So the times I sense Rena isn't doing well are actually when she's performing the hardest.@k@vS14/01/210100786. Especially lately. You remember how she was making an absurd amount of meatball lunches?``

``...I can't say for certain, but it looked to me like she was trying to neutralize her loneliness by strengthening her bonds with other people.@k@vS14/01/210100788. Which made me wonder if something had happened at home.``
``That's... quite complex.``
``...Back when I lived in Tokyo,@k@vS14/01/210100790. I knew a kid like that.``
``He'd suddenly show up with manga or toys without any prior arrangement, and we'd have a blast together... but there was always this undercurrent of desperation to it.``
``I found out later that every time he acted like that, he'd had a fight or some kind of trouble with his parents the day before.``
...When you're feeling insecure, perhaps you need to confirm it -- your own place in the world.
``Mii. Is that really how it works...?``

``...I bet Satoko picked up on it too, don't you think?@k@vS14/01/210100794. Otherwise, she wouldn't have suddenly asked to sleep over at Rena's house.@k@vS14/01/210100795. We've all been concerned, you know.``
Guilt-stricken, I hung my head. ...I was ashamed to admit it, but I hadn't noticed any of this. I'd simply assumed Satoko's sleepover was a spur-of-the-moment whim.
Everyone was thinking so carefully about these things. Genuinely caring about their friends, working their hardest to be there for them.
...Up until now, I'd repeated thousands upon thousands of encounters, but had I ever truly tried to understand my friends like this?
``That's amazing... both Keiichi and Satoko.@k@vS14/05/210500558. I need to learn from your example.``

``Well, it's hardly worth that kind of praise!@k@vS14/01/210100797. But no matter what happens, I'll help with everything I've got.@k@vS14/01/210100798. ...That's what being friends is all about, right?``
Keiichi said that with a smile. ...Loath as I was to admit it, the Keiichi of this world was far more grown-up than I was.
``So, the question is how best to break this to Rena.@k@vS14/01/210100800. Hmm, let me think...``
``...We could also wait for her to trust us enough to come to us on her own.``
I gently offered the suggestion to the troubled Keiichi.
In fact, in a previous world, the problem had been solved when Rena went to Mion to talk things over.
If Rena still had genuine trust in her friends, then all our worrying right now would be for nothing.
Or perhaps we'd just be stirring up a hornet's nest...
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content from the original, indicating a misaligned or completely different passage.

TextENGNew: ``True enough.@k@vS14/01/210100802. That's certainly one approach.``
``Rena is our friend.@k@vS14/05/210500561. And if she's our friend, I think we should trust her more.``
But Keiichi shook his head in disagreement.

``...You know, when it comes to opening up, reaching out is way harder than being reached out to.@k@vS14/01/210100804. So if Rena holds back out of consideration, makes her own decisions in isolation, and spirals out of control...``
``It could reach a point of no return, couldn't it?``
Keiichi's words struck me like a bolt of lightning.
It was exactly the same as what happened with Satoshi last year. Back then, we'd made the unilateral decision to wait until Satoshi came to us. ...In other words, we did nothing.
And what was the result? Satoshi lost control, fell under suspicion for his aunt's murder... and then vanished. In every sense, it was the worst possible outcome.
I hate words like 'what if' and 'maybe.'
But if we had just reached out to Satoshi, even if it had been meddlesome, if we'd paid closer attention... at the very least, maybe he wouldn't have felt driven to disappear.
No, it wasn't just that. The same was true of Rena in other worlds.
Back then, Rena told us:
``...When someone is truly suffering, they're desperate!``
...And who was it that had driven Rena to that point?
Keiichi? Mion? Satoko? ...Or me?
I could tell something was troubling Rena and dragging her spirits down, but I had no idea she was being cornered by family problems to that extent.
...By the time I understood that, it was already over. There was no turning back.
Human beings are not that strong.
...Which is why, even if someone wants to be saved, the more patient and kindhearted they are, the more determination and courage it takes for them to find the right moment and take that first step.
If you never create that opening for them... if you just sit back and pretend to care while waiting idly... no one is going to open their heart to you.
After all, even I myself...
It was only because of Keiichi's almost insufferably persistent meddling that I'd decided I could open up to him, wasn't it?
``I...@k@vS14/05/210500564. still haven't done enough...``
I murmured that... and let my head droop.
Reborn thousands of times, repeating the same conversations and events like a broken record.

...And somewhere along the way, the moment I could predict how things would unfold, I'd stopped fighting. I'd tell myself 'it's pointless anyway' and resign myself to acceptance... perhaps missing precious possibilities all along.
``...Rika-chan.``
A hand settled gently on my head, and Keiichi softly stroked my hair.
A warm, comforting sensation filtered through my hair. ...My cheeks grew just a little bit warm.
``Keiichi...``

``If there's something we can do, let's give it everything we've got.@k@vS14/01/210100808. The results will follow in time...@k@vS14/01/210100809. And even if we fail, we'll just think of another way.``
Keiichi said that with a smile. Before I knew it, I found myself smiling too.
Truly... you are an unbelievably kind person.
The next day, after school.
We decided to ask Rena if we could visit her house, using the pretext of wanting to see her prized collection.
``Huh...? That's fine, I suppose?@k@vS14/02/210200151. But what's with the sudden request? The request?``
Rena tilted her head at the unexpected invitation, but then Satoko jumped in at just the right moment.
``Ohohohoho!@k@vS14/04/210400220. I'm sure Rika heard me talking about it and got envious~!``
...Come to think of it, Satoko had stayed over at Rena's house last Sunday.
``Everything was even more upgraded than the last time I saw it!@k@vS14/04/VT4Ba_satoko1037. That traffic light in particular -- however did you manage to get your hands on one?``

``Ahahaha!@k@vS14/02/210200153. I recently discovered a spot at the junkyard absolutely overflowing with treasures~!@k@vS14/02/210200154. I'm going hunting again this weekend -- want to come, Satoko-chan? Come along?``
``That does sound delightful~!@k@vS14/04/210400224. I shall acquire a rare treasure that puts all of yours to shame!``
``H-hau~, no way~!@k@vS14/02/210200156. All the kyute stuff is for Rena to take home~!``
The two of them were chatting away happily. Thanks to this warm atmosphere, getting Rena's okay for a visit was even easier than expected. ...Nice one, Satoko.
``...I hear that place is haunted, so it's best to stay away.@k@vS14/05/210500566_2. ...Nipah~.``
``D-don't say things like that, it's bad luck...``
``But I'd love to see Rena's collection again too -- it's been ages.@k@vS14/03/210300441. Mind if I tag along, Kei-chan?``
``...Mii has a Watanagashi planning meeting after this.@k@vS14/05/210500568. So you won't be able to come along.``
``Urk... H-how did you know about that, Rika-chan?@k@vS14/03/210300443. Did Oyashiro-sama tell you?``
``It came through on the divine frequency. Nipah~.``

...In reality, I'd confirmed the schedule with Kiichirou. I'd actually suggested that Mion join us for today's visit to the Ryuugu house, but Keiichi had shot the idea down. ...Probably because she can't read the room.
``Well, next time for you, Mion.@k@vS14/01/210100811. Besides, I want to check on Randy-kun -- I put my heart and soul into excavating that guy!``
``Hau~, he's doing great, just great~!@k@vS14/02/210200158. I even take him to bed with me sometimes~!``
Was Rena seriously sleeping with that white-bearded old man statue at her bedside?
...Personally, I'd rather not have that face staring at me the moment I wake up. I can see it seriously ruining my mornings.
``So, Rika-chan, we'll meet up at Rena's house after heading home. Let's go!``
``Let's go! Nipah~.``
``Ahahaha!@k@vS14/02/210200160. Then I'll have tea and snacks ready for you, okay~?``
We were all getting excited. Mion, on the other hand, was left behind and pouting.
``...Boo hoo, Kei-chan's been spending all his time with Rika-chan lately.@k@vS14/03/210300445. This old man is getting bored~.``
``Ohohohoho!@k@vS14/04/210400226. Infidelity is the mark of a true man~!``
``......But if you're serious, I will not forgive you.``
``Ahahahaha!@k@vS14/02/210200162. Don't worry, Mii-chan. Keiichi-kun will always come back to you in the end.``
``......Right, Keiichi-kun?``
``Hold on, hold on!@k@vS14/01/210100814. First of all, forget cheating -- what is this default assumption you all have?!``
``How many times do I have to say it -- Mion and I are not like that!@k@vS14/01/210100816. And Rena, Satoko, your eyes look seriously terrifying!!``
``...Heyyy, Mion!@k@vS14/01/210100818. Don't just turn beet red -- at least try denying it a little!!``
...So that makes me Keiichi's mistress. How amusing.
``Mii~! Young fruit trumps mature women every time. Nipah~!``
``H-h-h-how filthy of you, Keiichi-kun!!@k@vS14/02/210200165. You need to be punished~~!!``
``R-Rena, gwah?!``
``W-wait a -- hngh?!``
``L-listen to me, argh?!``
``W-w-w-w-whoooo are you calling a mature woman?!@k@vS14/03/210300447. An old lady~~!!``
``......Then maybe stop calling yourself 'this old man.'``
``And where did you pop up from, Shiooon~~!!``
``Why, right here, of course!@k@vS14/06/210600389. I'm here again for Satoko's special training.@k@vS14/06/210600390. Today's creation: bacon-wrapped asparagus...``
``N-noooooo!@k@vS14/04/210400229. I told you before, asparagus is absolutely out of the question!!``
Our club members were as lively and fun as ever today.
......Which is why I wanted to protect this. Why I didn't want to lose it.
With that thought in my heart, I prayed that my feelings would reach Rena... praying to a 'god' who was no longer at my side.
Standing before the entrance to Rena's house, a trembling tension coursed through my entire body.
Every step of this approach would shape our fate. I closed my eyes as if in prayer.
Rena... no matter what happens, we're your friends...
I swear -- we will avert this tragedy...!
``......All right.``
I opened my eyes again and nodded to Keiichi beside me. His expression had shifted completely from the relaxed atmosphere of the classroom -- serious and silent, he nodded back.
I absolutely, positively had to prevent Rena from falling into tragedy...
Steeling myself, I pressed the doorbell at Rena's front entrance.
The chime echoed through the house, audible even from outside. Simultaneously, pattering footsteps in slippers approached from within.
The door opened, and Rena greeted us with a beaming smile.
``Keiichi-kun, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/02/210200167. Welcome!``
Rena was still wearing her apron, evidently having been doing housework in the kitchen. From the look of things, she'd been preparing everything for our arrival.
``Hey, we're coming in~.``
``Pardon the intrusion.``
``Sure thing -- come on in, come on in~!``
Rena lined up slippers for us and ushered us inside.
The warmth and openness radiated from every corner... and I started feeling a pang of guilt.
Thinking about what we were about to discuss with Rena filled me with dread. What we had come to say was anything but pleasant.
...If anything, it would only cause her grief.

As I slipped into the floral-patterned slippers Rena had set out, I gripped the hem of my skirt tight. If I could avoid this conversation entirely, I would. ...But we couldn't just leave without saying anything.
...This is for everyone's sake. And of course, for Rena's too. I repeated that to myself over and over.
Rena showed us to her room and brought out cookies and juice. The star-shaped cookies had a charming, unmistakably homemade quality to them.
``How are the cookies? The cookies?``
I munched on a cookie. Rena stared at me intently, as if awaiting the verdict.
``Nipah~! They're really delicious.``
I answered with a genuine smile. Rena lit up with happiness and threw her hands in the air.
``Yay~!@k@vS14/02/210200171. It's the ultimate Rena Special!@k@vS14/02/210200172. I hope you eat lots and lots! Wait right here!``
Rena pushed a heaping plate of cookies in front of us, then dashed out of the room in a flash. ...Was it my imagination, or was there an overwhelmingly sweet aroma wafting from beyond the door...?
``These really are... good... but...``
Keiichi muttered under his breath, and a flicker of unease crossed my mind too.
No way... could it be...
*clatter!* The door swung open again, and sure enough -- a mountain of cookies towered beyond the doorway...
N-no, it wasn't just a mountain... Rena was standing there holding a tray piled absurdly high with cookies.
``Thanks for waiting!@k@vS14/02/210200174. Actually, I've been baking a ton more!@k@vS14/02/210200175. Please eat as much as you want!``
``These ones are chocolate, these are mint, and these have almonds!``

``And these are little crab cookies.@k@vS14/02/210200178. See how the crab shape has these cute little pincers? Isn't it kyute?@k@vS14/02/210200179. And these are squid cookies!@k@vS14/02/210200180. Their legs are all wiggly!``
Rena was absolutely thrilled describing her special cookies.
Watching Rena like that... I found myself utterly unable to bring up the subject of her father...
In this warm, peaceful atmosphere, what was I supposed to do...?
``...Sorry, Rena.``
I'd been nibbling at cookies and staring downward, searching for an opening, when--
``The truth is, we came here because we have something to tell you.``
Keiichi cut cleanly through the comfortable atmosphere. Rena's expression immediately shifted to puzzlement at his firm tone.
``Huh?@k@vS14/02/210200182. What is it? ...What is it?``
``It's about your father.``
Rena's face clouded over instantly. ...Of course it did. Anyone would feel anxious if someone suddenly said they needed to talk about their father.
But this was for everyone's sake. For Rena's sake. ...For everyone's happiness.
Deep in my heart, I thanked Keiichi for taking the lead in talking to Rena.
``Actually... we heard something from Shion. About someone who seems to be your father...``
``...From Shii-chan?@k@vS14/02/210200185. What could it be? ...What could it be?``
Rena's eyes wavered with unease. From the looks on Keiichi's face and mine, she must have realized it wasn't good news.
It was then that I put a question to Rena.
``...What's this all of a sudden?@k@vS14/02/210200187. Why are you asking about that?``
``There are rumors circulating in the village.@k@vS14/01/210100830. That... the woman he's seeing works at a club.``
Rena's tone went flat and curt. A faint anger was visible in her eyes as she stared Keiichi down.
``Y-yes... I know about her.@k@vS14/02/210200190. What about it...?``
``That woman... she works at a club, right?@k@vS14/01/210100832. What I mean is--``
``......That's irrelevant.``
Rena cut in with a sharp, flat tone. There was a glint of anger in her eyes as she stared back at Keiichi.
``It doesn't matter where she works.@k@vS14/02/210200193. Whatever her occupation, my father likes her, and I think that should be enough.@k@vS14/02/210200194. ......Wouldn't you agree?``
``No, I wouldn't disagree with that.@k@vS14/01/210100834. ......If she were a decent person.``
``......What's that supposed to mean?``
The look in Rena's eyes was growing more and more dangerous.
...Something was off. It certainly wasn't a pleasant topic to hear about, but... Rena's reaction was strangely hostile. So I spoke up, even though I thought it might not be my place.
``...Even if you call her a bad person, I don't really understand how she's bad.@k@vS14/02/210200197. What exactly are you trying to say? ...Trying to say?``
``The rumors I've heard aren't good at all.@k@vS14/05/210500577. ...Not about her work as a hostess, but about her everyday conduct.``
Rena fell silent, grabbed a cookie, and bit into it. After finishing it, she spoke in a low murmur.
``......Rina-san is pretty flashy, I know.@k@vS14/02/210200200. But.``
``Rena's house... doesn't have a mom.``
I'd heard this story before, in some other iteration. Wondering how things stood in this world, I glanced up at Keiichi.
...He seemed to catch his breath slightly. He apparently hadn't known.
``'Why do you ask'...... What do you mean by that, Rika-chan?``
``I mean exactly what I said.@k@vS14/05/210500581. ...Rena, please answer me honestly.``
Rena fell silent, grabbed a cookie, and bit into it. After finishing it, she spoke in a low murmur.
``......I don't like her very much.@k@vS14/02/210200205. But.``
``Rena's house... doesn't have a mom.``
I'd heard this story before, in some other iteration. Wondering how things stood in this world, I glanced up at Keiichi.
...He seemed to catch his breath slightly. He apparently hadn't known.

``Back when we lived in another town,@k@vS14/02/210200208. Mom found another man she liked... and they divorced.@k@vS14/02/210200209. So selfish of her.@k@vS14/02/210200210. Even though Dad always supported her while she pursued the career she loved.``
``Of course I know he frequents places full of women.@k@vS14/02/210200212. ...But I think Dad is lonely.@k@vS14/02/210200213. He was suddenly all alone.``
Rena smiled sadly as she defended her father.

``He's lonely, so what can you do?@k@vS14/02/210200215. Rena doesn't think there's anything wrong with him going to places like that.@k@vS14/02/210200216. And actually, Dad has been so much happier since meeting Rina-san.``
``Right, there's nothing wrong with that.@k@vS14/01/210100837. But...``
``But -- what?``
As Keiichi fumbled for the right words, open hostility surfaced in Rena's eyes.
...This was wrong. It was as if she was rejecting everything Keiichi said outright. I never expected a reaction like this.

``Dad...@k@vS14/02/210200219. told me he's going to get married.@k@vS14/02/210200220. He already gave her an engagement ring, and she accepted it.@k@vS14/02/210200221. Rina-san even came to introduce herself and told me with a smile, 'I'll do my very best to get along with you.'``
``...Seriously?``

``Why would I lie about that?@k@vS14/02/210200223. Both Rina-san and Dad are going out of their way to be considerate of me.@k@vS14/02/210200224. So Rena thinks she should try to be patient for their sake.``
``...Is that so strange?@k@vS14/02/210200226. Am I wrong?``
``No... you're not.``
``...Then why, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS14/02/210200228. Did you come all the way to Rena's house just to say something like that?``
``Everyone's trying so hard to make it work, and you came to tear it all apart?@k@vS14/02/210200230. ...Were you too nervous to do it alone, so you brought Rika-chan for backup?``
Rena fixed Keiichi with a contemptuous stare.
...No, that wasn't right. Rena had been misunderstanding something this whole time. Sensing that, I hastily spoke up.
``...I'm not angry.@k@vS14/02/210200232. Please don't make wild accusations, Rika-chan.``
``The way you're saying that proves you are angry!``
Rena's eyes wavered, ever so slightly.
But she shot a sharp glance at Keiichi, then looked away with an irritated expression, fidgeting with her hair.
...Why? Rena, why are you looking at us like that...?
I tried to continue, but Keiichi held up a hand to stop me. Then he pressed on.
``...Rika-chan? Why...?``
``Something I heard from Shii made me worried.@k@vS14/05/210500585. About this woman, Rina Mamiya...``
Rena's eyes wavered, ever so slightly.
But the hostility-tinged gaze that had been aimed at Keiichi now turned toward me as well.
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content with different character references, indicating a misaligned passage.

TextENGNew: ...Why? Rena, why are you looking at us like that...?
I tried to continue, but Keiichi held up a hand to stop me.
``Rena, how much do you actually know about this Rina woman?``
``I already told you, didn't I? It doesn't matter.@k@vS14/02/210200237. So there's nothing to discuss.``
``Then I'll just talk on my own.@k@vS14/01/210100843. ...That's all right, isn't it?``
``...Even if you talk, I won't listen.@k@vS14/02/210200239. But if you insist on speaking, be my guest.``
Rena pointedly turned away as she said that. Even so, Keiichi remained calm and began speaking slowly, as though addressing her regardless.
``I heard this from Shion.@k@vS14/01/210100845. ...Rena, your father has been visiting the club where Rina Mamiya works on a regular basis.@k@vS14/01/210100846. Almost every single day.``

``Apparently that place is one of the most expensive in the entertainment district.@k@vS14/01/210100848. A single visit costs a staggering amount.@k@vS14/01/210100849. ...Which is why regulars are quickly recognized, I'm told.``
``Stop talking like that.``
``...I thought you weren't listening?@k@vS14/01/210100851. I'm just talking to myself here.``
Rena reached for her cup and drained the now-lukewarm tea. Then she held the empty cup in both hands, silently staring into its depths.
``I'll say it plainly.@k@vS14/01/210100853. ...Rena, Rina Mamiya is a con artist.@k@vS14/01/210100854. She's been running marriage fraud schemes with a male partner.``
``Word is Rina's been bragging about finding an easy mark in Hinamizawa.@k@vS14/01/210100856. And the surname that came up in those conversations... was 'Ryuugu.'``
``......Where's your proof?``

``This also came from Shion.@k@vS14/01/210100858. Rina finds well-off men to sweet-talk, then squeezes large sums of money and gifts out of them.@k@vS14/01/210100859. She's apparently become quite infamous for it, even at that particular club.``
``......They're adults, so of course gifts will be expensive.@k@vS14/02/210200246. Isn't my father free to spend as he chooses?``

``Then let me ask you straight.@k@vS14/01/210100861. ...Is it normal to make someone you're supposedly planning to marry spend that kind of money?@k@vS14/01/210100862. And if I were in that kind of relationship with you, Rena--``
``...Absolutely not.@k@vS14/02/210200248. Rena has the right to choose too.@k@vS14/02/210200249. I would never want to marry someone who meddles and says all these horrible things.``
Rena spat those words with an openly disgusted expression. Keiichi let out a single sigh and rephrased his point.

``Fine, then substitute Mion.@k@vS14/01/210100864. Or Satoko, or Rika-chan.@k@vS14/01/210100865. ...If I were serious about marrying any of them, I wouldn't let my partner keep coming to a place like that.``
``Thinking about how my partner would feel -- it's not just the money, I'd hate for them to suspect I was seeing other women.``
``...Normal workplaces have men and women too, you know.@k@vS14/02/210200251. If you took that to its logical conclusion, nobody could get married and hold a job.``
``This isn't a normal workplace, though.@k@vS14/01/210100868. We're talking about an establishment where you pay to enjoy the fantasy of romance.@k@vS14/01/210100869. With money.``
``......I already told you, it's because Dad is lonely.``
``I'll say it one more time, Rena.@k@vS14/01/210100871. ...Your father is being deceived.@k@vS14/01/210100872. By Rina Mamiya--``
``That's a LIE!!!``
*crash!* Rena slammed her cup down on the table with violent force. The mountain of cookies wobbled and spilled everywhere.
``I've been sitting here listening quietly, and all you do is spout whatever you please!``
``Keiichi-kun, do you think Rena's Dad is some kind of idiot?!@k@vS14/02/210200256. He wouldn't propose to someone without being able to tell whether she's a scammer!!``
``What are you saying?!@k@vS14/01/210100874. The whole point of a marriage scam is that the marriage itself is the deception!``

``On top of that, she's connected to a man named Teppei Houjou!@k@vS14/01/210100876. ...And besides, you're the one who's been acting strange this whole time, Rena!@k@vS14/01/210100877. Shion went out of her way to share this information with us -- why are you dismissing it without even listening?!``

``Strange?@k@vS14/02/210200258. ...You're the one being strange, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS14/02/210200259. Why are you butting into Rena's family business when it has nothing to do with you?@k@vS14/02/210200260. Did Shii-chan put you up to this?``
``Well?!``
``Nothing to do with me?!@k@vS14/01/210100879. You're our friend, Rena!@k@vS14/01/210100880. I can't just stand by and watch a friend suffer!``
``Friends?@k@vS14/02/210200263. ...Ha! Don't make me laugh!@k@vS14/02/210200264. Someone who stabs a knife into your wounds and twists it without a thought for how you feel is no friend!``
``And who's worried?@k@vS14/02/210200266. Who's suffering?@k@vS14/02/210200267. Ahahahaha!``

``You've sure gotten full of yourself since becoming class representative, haven't you, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS14/02/210200269. What, you think you can read people just from their faces and the mood of the room?``
``Well, too bad for you!@k@vS14/02/210200271. Rena isn't worried or suffering one bit!@k@vS14/02/210200272. Because I'm going to seize my happiness with my own hands!!``
``You're lying!!``
``I am not!!``
...That's right.

No matter how stubborn Rena could be, this was abnormal. She wasn't so much defending her father as flat-out refusing to acknowledge anything Keiichi said -- as if she couldn't allow herself to accept it.
Moreover, the information came from Shion.
It should have been as reliable and well-intentioned as information could possibly be. ...And yet Rena was dismissing it as 'lies' -- information from her own friends.
This was exactly like Rena had been when she caused that tragedy.
``...Rena.@k@vS14/05/210500587. Do you not believe what Shii told us?``
``...I have no idea what Shii-chan was thinking, badmouthing Rina-san like that.``
``But don't say strange things to confuse me!@k@vS14/02/210200276. I've made my decision -- to believe in this marriage and give it my blessing.@k@vS14/02/210200277. This has already been decided!``
``Already... decided?``
``Because this is--!``
``...Because Oyashiro-sama told me to!!``
Rena declared that... with a forceful, unequivocal finality.
``Oyashiro-sama, you said...?``
An unfamiliar word. ...No, wait. I remembered now. It was the name of Hinamizawa's guardian deity, enshrined at the upcoming Watanagashi festival.
But why would she bring up Oyashiro-sama...?
``...Rika-chan?``
I instinctively turned to Rika-chan beside me, but she looked just as blindsided -- her eyes wide, her expression bewildered.

``At first, Rena wasn't happy about Dad's marriage either.@k@vS14/02/210200281. But one night about a week ago... it came to my bedside.@k@vS14/02/210200282. With those tap-tap, pat-pat footsteps...``

As she spoke, Rena clasped her hands against her chest and trembled faintly. Her face was rigid with fear, and she was clearly not joking. Sensing how deeply unsettling this was, I straightened up and faced her.
``Why would... Oyashiro-sama come to you, Rena...?``

``You don't know, do you, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS14/02/210200284. ...Oyashiro-sama is always watching us.@k@vS14/02/210200285. Cursing and punishing those who leave Hinamizawa or fail to show proper reverence.``
``So Rena's family was punished too... because we didn't honor Oyashiro-sama enough...!``
Rena curled in on herself and muttered as though delirious. Her words were bizarre, outlandish... they didn't sound like those of a sane person. But I kept quiet and listened.

``...Mom and Dad's divorce was Rena's fault.@k@vS14/02/210200288. When we left Hinamizawa, Rena was against it.@k@vS14/02/210200289. But they told me it was for Mom's sake, so I went along... and Dad ended up unhappy.``
``...If only I'd fought harder, if only I'd thought more about Dad's happiness, this would never have happened...!``
``...What does this have to do with the remarriage?``

``Everything!@k@vS14/02/210200292. Dad finally managed to put his old wounds behind him and find new happiness.@k@vS14/02/210200293. And yet Rena couldn't bring herself to be happy about it.@k@vS14/02/210200294. I couldn't bring myself to support it.``
``Gradually, Rina-san became an eyesore, just looking at her made me uncomfortable, I started thinking she was trying to take over my house, and before that could happen, I should--!``
``...And that's when it happened again!@k@vS14/02/210200297. When Oyashiro-sama came to Rena!``
Rena was visibly trembling, shaking so hard it was plain to see how terrified she was. Rika-chan rushed to her side and wrapped her small arms around her in a tight embrace.
``...Oyashiro-sama is not that kind of god.@k@vS14/05/210500589. Even if you left Hinamizawa, a true god would never curse an innocent person.``
``But even now, it still appears in my dreams sometimes!``
Tears spilled from Rena's eyes as she cried out. Wrapping both arms around her own chest, still trembling, she continued speaking as if she could no longer even see me.
``Oyashiro-sama told me.@k@vS14/02/210200300. To cast away my hostility.@k@vS14/02/210200301. To sincerely wish for Dad's happiness.``

``That's why Rena is giving her blessing to the marriage with Rina-san.@k@vS14/02/210200303. Otherwise, I'll be cursed again!@k@vS14/02/210200304. Otherwise, they'll make me take that strange-colored medicine again!``
changeReason: Narrative perspective shifts from third person to first person.

TextENGNew: ``If Rena's selfish feelings ruin the happiness Dad finally found, Oyashiro-sama will come for me again...!``

``Blaming me, calling me a bad girl, following behind me with those tap-tap, tap-tap footsteps!!@k@vS14/02/210200310. No matter how many times I say I'm sorry, those tap-tap, pat-pat sounds just keep following me!!``
``Calm down, Rena...!@k@vS14/05/210500591. There's no one there.@k@vS14/05/210500592. You haven't done anything wrong, so you won't be cursed...!``
``Hurry, hurry!@k@vS14/02/210200312. If I don't let go of these feelings quickly... Oyashiro-sama's power will fade, and something will take over my body again...!!``

``Aah, Oyashiro-sama is following me, whispering 'hurry, hurry'!@k@vS14/02/210200314. Following me everywhere, urging me to rush, rush!@k@vS14/02/210200315. Following me forever and ever, saying it over and over!!``
``It's true!@k@vS14/02/210200317. I'm telling you, it's true!!@k@vS14/02/210200318. Why can't any of you see it?!@k@vS14/02/210200319. Why won't anyone believe what I'm saying?!``
``You're all deceiving me! You all must be able to see it too! No, no, stop it -- no more injections, please, nooooo...!!!``
``Haah, haah, haah, haah...!``
Rena's ragged breathing gradually steadied as she went limp in our arms, finally calming down as Keiichi and I held her.
``......You okay now, Rena...?``
``......Rena, are you all right?``
A tiny, faint nod from Rena.
Her whole body was drenched in sweat and still trembling in small spasms. ...Even so, the frenzied look from before had receded, and Keiichi and I exchanged a glance and sighed with relief.
``......I'm sorry...@k@vS14/02/210200325. I... lost control of myself...``
``...Hey, it's okay.@k@vS14/01/210100891. This is nothing.``
Rika-chan gazed up at Rena with worry and didn't loosen her embrace one bit. Perhaps that earnest sincerity reached Rena, because her face slowly began to flush.

``......You probably won't believe me, but... Rena really did see Oyashiro-sama.@k@vS14/02/210200327. So when it returned, I was so frightened and anxious, I felt like running away, and my mind just went blank...@k@vS14/02/210200328. So... I just...``
``......I understand.``
At my words of acceptance, Rena instinctively looked up at me.
Even I thought it might have sounded too readily given.
But.
I could say with certainty that those words weren't some offhand platitude. They came from the heart.
Fear, anxiety... suffering so intense it makes you want to flee. I believed I understood, at least a little, how hard it was to find the courage to step forward from that place...
``There's no way you'd lie about something like this, Rena.@k@vS14/01/210100894. And whatever's scaring you this badly can't be dismissed as just a dream.@k@vS14/01/210100895. ...I believe you.``

``If Oyashiro-sama truly said that, then I suppose it can't be helped.@k@vS14/01/210100897. ...I'm sorry for saying such awful things and upsetting you.@k@vS14/01/210100898. I really am sorry... Let's drop this subject.``
``K-Keiichi-kun...``
``Keiichi--``
Rena and Rika-chan both gazed at me, each wearing a complicated expression.
...Shion's information was almost certainly true. But pressing the truth and the logic of the situation didn't necessarily lead to happiness.
For Rena, Oyashiro-sama's existence was absolute. It was her worldview, her moral compass.
...If that was the case, then rather than denying it and forcing my own values on her, helping her find peace within the boundaries of that belief -- wasn't that the role of a true friend?
...That was what I thought.
Just then, I heard the roar of a large engine outside. It grew thunderously loud as it approached and stopped right in front of Rena's house, accompanied by a faint rumbling of the ground beneath it.
*Ding-dong!* *Ding-dong, ding-dong!*
``Huh...? I wonder what that is.@k@vS14/02/210200333. ...Sorry, let me go check.``
Rena dabbed the tears from her reddened eyes, then rose to her feet and left the room. Rika-chan and I watched her go in silence.
``Excuse me!@k@vS14/00/unso10002. Where would you like me to bring these packages?``
The hearty voice of what sounded like a delivery driver echoed all the way up to the second floor. ...As we listened to it in passing, Rika-chan and I exchanged glances.
``——Keiichi.``
``I know.@k@vS14/01/210100900. ...But pushing any harder than this would just be forcing ourselves on her.``
Rika-chan let out a sigh and let her shoulders droop. ...Truth be told, I hadn't expected to be rejected so completely either, so I couldn't suppress a feeling of deep disappointment.
``For now... let's call it a day and head home.@k@vS14/01/210100902. If we keep pressing, we'll only end up upsetting Rena again.``
``...It can't be helped.@k@vS14/05/210500599. Let's regroup for now... and come up with a new plan.``
